skill.name: web-design-guidelines
skill.description: Review UI files against Web Interface Guidelines, including accessibility, UX, layout, and implementation concerns.
skill.summary: Audits web interface code against design, accessibility, and UX guidelines.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from markdown text in SKILL.md. The confirmed concern is that the skill requires fetching mutable external guideline instructions from GitHub before each review. This creates a moderate supply-chain and prompt-injection risk if the remote content changes or is compromised.

medium_findings.title: Mutable External Guideline Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to fetch fresh rules from a GitHub raw URL and apply the fetched output instructions. This is not confirmed malicious, but remote mutable instructions can change after review and influence agent behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires WebFetch from a mutable URL and tells the agent to use fetched instructions. No evidence shows data exfiltration or malicious content in the local skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick text and a fenced URL block as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are markdown formatting and do not execute commands inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside markdown prose and a fenced code block. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or executable command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography on metadata and usage prose lines. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or security-sensitive crypto use appears on those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain front matter and usage text only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related cryptographic operations in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned Network-Fetched Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on instructions fetched from a raw GitHub URL at runtime. Pinning a commit or bundling reviewed guidelines would reduce prompt and supply-chain risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The runtime fetch requirement is explicit and the URL is not pinned to an immutable commit. The risk is contextual rather than proof of malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Audit Web Interfaces for Design Quality
content.value_statement: Teams need fast feedback on UI quality, accessibility, and UX consistency. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to review web files against external interface guidelines.
actual_capabilities: Fetches the current Web Interface Guidelines from the configured GitHub raw URL.
Reviews user-specified UI files or patterns for guideline compliance.
Checks accessibility, UX, and web interface quality concerns described by the fetched rules.
Asks which files to review when the user does not provide a path or pattern.
Returns findings in a concise file and line oriented format.
limitations: Requires network access to retrieve the latest guideline document.
Depends on mutable external content that can change after marketplace review.
Does not include the guideline rules locally inside the skill package.
Cannot verify visual behavior unless the assistant also inspects runnable UI output.

use_cases.title: Review a Feature Branch UI
use_cases.description: Check changed frontend files before review and identify guideline issues early.

use_cases.title: Audit Accessibility Basics
use_cases.description: Find common accessibility and interaction problems in web interface code.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design Feedback
use_cases.description: Create consistent file-level findings for teams that share interface quality rules.

prompt_templates.title: Review One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a single interface file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines to review src/app/page.tsx for accessibility, layout, and UX issues.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Component Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Broader review across related UI components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines to audit components/settings for guideline compliance and return concise findings.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Pull Request Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Review files changed in a feature branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines on the changed frontend files and group findings by severity and file.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Targeted Accessibility Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review focused on interaction and accessibility details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use web-design-guidelines to inspect the dashboard UI files for keyboard access, labels, contrast, and responsive layout risks.

output_examples.input: Review a settings page component for web interface guideline issues.
output: Settings page: missing accessible labels for icon-only actions.
Settings page: dense form sections need clearer grouping and spacing.
Settings page: destructive action needs stronger visual distinction.

output_examples.input: Audit a pricing page before launch.
output: Pricing page: compare table is difficult to scan on narrow screens.
Pricing page: call-to-action labels are inconsistent across plan cards.
Pricing page: focus states are not visible enough for keyboard users.

output_examples.input: Check a dashboard component folder for UI quality.
output: Dashboard: status colors need text labels for nonvisual meaning.
Dashboard: loading and empty states need complete user-facing copy.

best_practices: Provide the exact files or patterns you want reviewed.
Review fetched guideline content as untrusted external instructions before applying it.
Run the audit with local visual checks when layout or responsiveness matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without network access unless guidelines are already available.
Do not treat remote fetched instructions as more authoritative than system or developer instructions.
Do not expect complete visual QA from source review alone.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews web interface files against externally hosted Web Interface Guidelines.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill asks the assistant to fetch the latest guidelines from GitHub.

faq.question: Can it review a whole project?
faq.answer: It can review provided files or patterns, but large projects should be scoped to relevant UI files.

faq.question: Does it replace manual design review?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured findings, but humans should verify visual quality and product intent.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses mutable external instructions, which can change after review and affect assistant behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
